zh.lifeSummary: 當Devorguilla de Burgh於大約1256年出生在英國賀特福爾賀特福德時，她的父親John de Burgh21歲，母親Cecily de Balliol20歲。她於大約1274年與Sir Robert FitzWalter 1st Lord FitzWalter結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1284年享年29歲，埋葬在英國艾可蕾托 登莫福。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Devorguilla
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Devorguilla de Burgh was born about 1256, in Hertford, Hertfordshire, England, her father, John de Burgh, was 21 and her mother, Cecily de Balliol, was 20. She married Sir Robert FitzWalter 1st Lord FitzWalter about 1274. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died in 1284, at the age of 29, and was buried in Little Dunmow, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Devorguilla de Burgh est née environ 1256, à Hertford, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, John de Burgh, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Cecily de Balliol, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Robert FitzWalter 1st Lord FitzWalter environ 1274. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1284, à l’âge de 29 ans, et a été enterrée à Little Dunmow, Essex, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Devorguilla de Burghが約1256年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアハートフォードで生まれた時，父John de Burghは21歳，母Cecily de Balliolは20歳であった。約1274年にSir Robert FitzWalter 1st Lord FitzWalterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1284年に29歳で死去し，イギリスエセックスリットル ダンモウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Devorguilla de Burgh(이)가 대략 1256년 영국 허트퍼드실르 허트퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Burgh(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Cecily de Balliol(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 대략 1274년 Sir Robert FitzWalter 1st Lord FitzWalter(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1284년 29세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 에세스 리틀 던모에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Devorguilla de Burgh родилась около 1256 г. в местности хиртфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John de Burgh было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Cecily de Balliol, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Robert FitzWalter 1st Lord FitzWalter около 1274 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1284 г. в возрасте 29 лет и была похоронена в местности литл дунмоу, эссекс, Англия.
